body_preview: - Trajectory shows 7 reverted_no_edit tasks across 5 recent sessions (Day 110-111 window). The root cause: the preseed picker selects tasks pointing at files that don't exist or are not part of the working tree. - Day 111 added a file-existence check to preseed_session_plan.py, but this only catches non-existent files — it doesn't catch gitignored files, gen
expected_evidence: Future sessions: reverted_no_edit task count drops to zero (or near-zero, excluding non-file-related reverts). State events: fewer TaskReverted events with "file not found" or "not in working tree" reasons. Dashboard: task_success_rate improves as fewer slots are wasted on unreachable files. Implementation Notes: Add a helper that checks `git ls-files --erro
title: Harden preseed task picker with git-tracked file check
